topic: atlas_health_timeline.jsonl size-threshold rotator + atlas_status_all defense/timeline section enrichment (Ω-cycle)

context.trigger: session-accumulating timeline file (107 lines, 28 KB) + new defenses (R1 cmd_sha256, TAMPERED status, R3-full hook, bridge_sha256.tsv) absent from atlas_status_all aggregator

decisions: Policy B chosen (size threshold) over A (unbounded) / C (daily) / D (TTL): predictable archive cadence, no time-clock dependency, simple recovery (one mv).
Default threshold 5000 lines (~500 KB typical given 280-byte avg JSONL); user-configurable via --threshold N.
Archive name `atlas_health_timeline.<UTC_ISO_compact>.jsonl.archive` so chronological sort matches lexical sort and `.archive` suffix shields from glob patterns scanning live timelines.
Single-line JSONL log entry per rotation in state/atlas_health_timeline.rotation.log (ts/old_lines/threshold/archive — replayable audit).
Don't auto-fire — user (or launchd/cron after explicit opt-in) invokes; ships a tool, not a daemon (raw 71 report+manual).
92-line bash 3.2 script under raw 73 admissibility cap (initial draft 101 → trimmed help/error to 92).
Sentinel format `__TIMELINE_ROTATE__ <ROTATED|SKIPPED|ERROR> threshold=N current=L action=...` (raw 80 — discoverable + grep-friendly + 3-state).
raw 66 reason+fix trailers on every error path: invalid threshold / unknown arg / missing file / mv failure / touch failure.
--check (dry-run) sentinel uses action=dry_run_would_rotate or under_threshold so cron schedulers can probe safely.
Sequence: mv first (atomic on same FS), then `: > $TIMELINE` to recreate empty file. mv-failed exits 1; touch-failed reports archive location for manual recovery.
Added section 7 (defense) and section 8 (timeline) before SUMMARY block — preserves existing 6-section visual order.
R1 falsifier shows first-12-char prefix (full 64-char hash too noisy in dashboard); R1 bridge counts non-comment TSV rows + extracts oldest last_verified_utc.
R3-full hook detection greps pre-commit for 'R3 full|R3-full|r3_full' so refactors don't desync — single source of truth in the hook header.
R4 rotation count tolerant of missing log file (defaults 0 — registry has not yet rotated locally).

Aggregate sentinel extended with 6 new fields: defense_r1_falsifier / defense_r1_bridge / defense_r3_full / defense_r4_rotations / timeline_lines / timeline_last_rot — preserves backward-compat (existing parsers ignore extra keys).

trials.name: low-threshold-dryrun
trials.result: __TIMELINE_ROTATE__ SKIPPED threshold=50 current=107 action=dry_run_would_rotate (with proposed archive path)

trials.name: status_all-aggregate
trials.result: sentinel: __ATLAS_STATUS_ALL__ runtime=stage_5 dashboard_h=3/4 facts=10022 ... defense_r1_falsifier=7fa050a99a72 defense_r1_bridge=16 defense_r3_full=yes defense_r4_rotations=0 timeline_lines=107 timeline_last_rot=-

tier_1: timeline_rotate.sh shipped (92 lines bash 3.2, 3 modes, sentinel + raw 66 trailers)
atlas_status_all.sh enriched: section 7 defense (R1 falsifier sha + R1 bridge count/oldest + R3-full hook + R4 rotation count), section 8 timeline (lines + last rotation ts)
Aggregator sentinel extended +6 fields (defense_r1_falsifier / defense_r1_bridge / defense_r3_full / defense_r4_rotations / timeline_lines / timeline_last_rot)
nexus CLI hexa-sim timeline-rotate subcommand wired in cli/run.hexa (help line + dispatch block)
tier_2: Policy B over A/C/D rationale captured (size threshold > daily/TTL for predictable cadence)
Default threshold 5000 lines = ~500 KB given current 280-byte avg JSONL line
Archive naming uses UTC compact ISO so lexical = chronological
tier_3: User has not yet rotated; rotation log absent — first rotation will materialize state/atlas_health_timeline.rotation.log
Future: launchd/cron timer (hourly --check + nightly real rotate) — deferred to user opt-in per raw 71

fixpoint.evidence: all 4 verification trials PASS; CLI dispatch reaches new tool through nexus hexa-sim namespace; aggregator sentinel parsed cleanly with 6 new fields; tool size 92 ≤ 100 (raw 73 OK)

deliverables.cli_diff: cli/run.hexa: +help line + dispatch block for `hexa-sim timeline-rotate`
